message:
refactor(mvcc/morsel_parallel_insert): simplify schedule() indexing math and condense test assertions

Follow-up to the just-landed morsel scheduling primitive. Two small
cleanups on the same file:

schedule() indexing
- Dropped the `worker_count.max(1)` coercion inside `schedule()`.
  `MorselScheduler::new` already rejects `worker_count = 0`, so the
  coercion was defense against a state the type can't be in.
- Inlined `bins[idx % worker_count]` directly instead of binding it
  to a `slot` local. The previous long comment justifying why
  `bins[idx % worker_count]` is in range unfolded from the original
  "slot" variable name; with the binding gone the reasoning reads
  as a single three-line proof alongside the loop, which is easier
  to re-audit on a future touch.
- Clarified the safety comment: `active = min(worker_count,
  morsels.len())`, and every residue `idx % worker_count` produced
  by `for (idx, morsel) in 0..morsels.len()` is in `0..active`,
  so direct indexing is sound without bounds handling.

Test condensation
- Shortened the round-robin unit test's verbose multi-arg
  `assert_eq!(..., "all 4 workers receive ...")` and per-worker
  format message into a single equality plus a one-line comment
  listing the expected round-robin assignments. The original 13-line
  expected-mapping comment read like a proof of the function body;
  the 1-line form `w0=[0,4,8], w1=[1,5,9], w2=[2,6], w3=[3,7]` is
  enough to read against the assertion, and the expected[] literal
  right below it remains the authoritative source.

Pure refactor — no behavior change in `schedule()` and no change in
what the test asserts about round-robin distribution.
